versions.label: BSB
paragraphs: 27 Now the Israelites settled in the land of Egypt, in the region of Goshen. They acquired property there and became fruitful and increased greatly in number. 28 And Jacob lived in the land of Egypt seventeen years, and the length of his life was 147 years.

29 When the time drew near for Israel to die, he called his son Joseph and said to him, “If I have found favor in your eyes, put your hand under my thigh and promise to show me kindness and faithfulness. Do not bury me in Egypt, 30 but when I lie down with my fathers, carry me out of Egypt and bury me with them.”
Joseph answered, “I will do as you have requested.”
So Joseph swore to him, and Israel bowed in worship at the head of his bed.

context_lines: In **Genesis 47**, this section should be read within the flow of the chapter and the larger movement of Genesis.
common_interpretation_lines: A common reading of **The Israelites Prosper in Goshen** is that it should be interpreted in light of the chapter's full narrative movement rather than as an isolated proof text.
Where interpreters differ on details, the immediate setting in **Genesis 47** remains the first control on meaning.
